common_structures: Single punchy sentence that reframes a concept
Before/after or then/now progression (A was X, then Y, now Z)
Binary contrast framing (not X vs Y, it's A vs B)
Short declarative followed by brief elaboration
Question as headline, answer implied or linked
Parallel list with a payoff line at the end
One-word or two-word affirmation with a link
topics_that_perform: AI reframing existing industries or job roles
Technology trend predictions stated as inevitable facts
Historical tech pattern mapped onto the present
Personal product experiences stated as broader signals
Geopolitics or policy with a sharp quantitative angle
things_to_avoid: Hedging language (maybe, possibly, it seems like)
Asking for engagement explicitly (like, retweet, comment)
Filler phrases like 'I think' or 'in my opinion'
style_tips: State the conclusion first, strip all throat-clearing — the entire insight should survive being cut to one sentence
Use structural contrast to create tension: old vs new, expected vs actual, junior vs senior — the gap is where the insight lives
Write like you are updating a proverb — timeless framing applied to a current trend makes ideas feel both urgent and durable
Compress a multi-paragraph argument into a single declarative sentence; if you need more, use a 3-line progression with a kicker
Pick a side hard — ambivalence kills engagement; the most liked posts make a claim that someone could strongly disagree with

top_tweet_samples.text: The human brain isn’t designed to process all of the world’s breaking emergencies in realtime.

A “computer” used to be a job title. 

Then a computer became a thing humans used. 

Now a computer is becoming a thing computers use.
Coding an app is the new starting a podcast.
AI is going to drain a lot of moats.
It’s not about junior vs senior, it’s about “good with AI” vs “not good with AI.”
Drones are easy to make, hard to stop, and bring Mutually Assured Destruction to conventional warfare.
The FBI cleared me. You on the other hand…

Software will proliferate just as videos, music,  writing did. 

The market structure will shift from a “fat middle” to mega-aggregators and a long tail.

It’ll be a slower process due to network effects, but many traditional vendor lock-ins will get eaten by AI.

I Fight For The Users: a post in support of migrating away from Twitter and generating funds for The Trevor Project. Please bid as you see fit for a good cause! https://t.co/WGTWMwPgqJ -- this will be my last post on Twitter 👋 https://t.co/lkjOn4ITvz
